new_classes.label_en: PGI wine in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (CN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.label_de: g.g.A.-Wein in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 l und bis 10 l (KN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI/g.g.A.) within the meaning of additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 29 79.

coverage_explanation: The two core discriminating criteria are captured: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI/g.g.A.) per additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, modelled via eucn:PGIWineQuality; and (2) the container volume is > 2 l and ≤ 10 l, modelled via eucn:MediumContainer2To10L (already present in the running TBox). The sibling code 22042980 also covers PGI wine in the same container band but is distinguished by a different subheading split (actual alcoholic strength or other criteria referenced in additional note 6(a)); however, the precise sub-criterion that separates 22042979 from 22042980 (e.g. actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15 % vol vs. > 15 % vol, or specific wine type) is not fully stated in the supplied legal note — it merely references additional note 6(a) generically. Without the full text of additional note 6(a), the discriminating factor between 22042979 and 22042980 cannot be completely encoded, reducing coverage to 0.85. The PGI quality and container size restriction together correctly exclude all non-PGI wines and all wines in containers ≤ 2 l or > 10 l.
